key_insight: A mesh-preserving attention surrogate can learn PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics on evolving geometries while remaining scalable and stable over long rollouts.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster engineering simulation of free-surface and non-Newtonian flows without abandoning finite-element mesh structure, so derived fields like stress can still be reconstructed.
method: Uses self-attention to model interactions between mesh nodes on arbitrary, changing geometries.
Compares standard self-attention with a linear attention variant for lower computational cost.
Keeps PFEM remeshing and node redistribution in the loop to preserve mesh quality during rollout.
evidence_signal: The model is evaluated on 2D and 3D free-surface flow benchmarks with evolving geometries and variable material properties, showing accurate transient and final-state predictions.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of remeshing and node redistribution, which can affect stability and accuracy in long simulations.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
